data.content_ref: inline:The military resilience of Egypt (3544632a92cf) is a specific instance of the general mechanism in 263738c8259b: a state's survival during the Late Bronze Age collapse depended on whether its administrative-military organization could absorb ironworking diffusion without triggering recursive legitimation crises. Egypt's temple-subsidized archer-based army was structurally compatible with iron adoption, unlike chariot-aristocracy-dependent states. REFUTED IF: Egypt's military organization was actually chariot-aristocracy dependent, or if iron adoption in Egypt did trigger a legitimation crisis comparable to other palace economies.
